'''Eliad''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of wealthy noble exiles in the [[Minos Cluster/Legends|Minos Cluster]].<ref name="GG6" />

==Description==
The climate and terrain of Eliad was extremely wide-ranging, with almost any biome being able to be found there.<ref name="GG6" /> Though few people lived on the planet compared to a core world, those few who did were exceedingly wealthy, and palaces, villas, and replica castles could be found widely across the planet.<ref name="GG6" /> The planet had little in trade aside from mineral exports; the immensely rich nobles, with absolutely nothing productive to spend their money on, instead engaged in increasingly sophisticated and demented entertainments out of sheer boredom.<ref name="GG6" /> Though separated from mainstream nobility, generations of family tradition and the highest quality education available in the galaxy made these nobles as intelligent and sophisticated as they were rich.<ref name="GG6" />

==History==
Prior to the [[Rebellion era]], the [[Battle of Eliad]] was a significant engagement of the [[Minos Cluster Campaign]] during the [[Great Galactic War]], with the planet being a noteworthy foothold strategically in the [[Minos Cluster]] as a whole.<ref name="JoMGD">[[The Journal of Master Gnost-Dural (real-life book)|''The Journal of Master Gnost-Dural'']]</ref>

Later, when [[Palpatine/Legends|Emperor Palpatine]] overthrew the [[Galactic Republic|Old Republic]] and declared his [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]], he removed mass numbers of nobility from power.<ref name="GG6" /> Instead of killing these nobles and making martyrs out of them, however, he made a bargain with them; if they agreed to permanent exile, he would allow them to keep much of their wealth and assumed status.<ref name="GG6" /> While many chose to flee or chose death rather than to submit, a large number of noble families accepted the deal, and in a mass exodus they were relocated out of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] and into the obscurity of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]].<ref name="GG6" /> In the end, three hundred thousand nobles were relocated to Eliad, along with their organic servants and massive numbers of [[Droid/Legends|droids]], where they were left to slowly and imperceptibly rot away under the weight of their own worthlessness and irrelevance - exactly as the Emeperor intended for them all along.<ref name="GG6" />

During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Rebel [[Agent/Legends|agents]] assisting [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia]] held many debate sessions to encourage these deposed nobles into assisting the Rebel Alliance. After many weeks the nobles agreed to assist the Alliance. With the much needed influx of wealth the Alliance was one more step closer to bringing peace and justice to [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="GG6" />

==Sources==
